As for our logger friend, apparently, he spends most of his time in the woods and hasn't heard of TrumpRX and the President's successful efforts to bring down the price of prescription medicines, or the efforts of RFK to remove harmful chemicals and additives from America's food supply. As for Mr. Shah, my question is 'How many more American citizens need to die at the hands of illegal immigrants?' I am saddened that anyone has lost their life, but the handful who have died because of unfortunate interactions with ICE pales in comparison to the many who have been murdered, raped, trafficked and abused by foreign nationals. It should be noted that, unlike those foreign nationals, ICE agents don't enter an engagement with a thirst to kill. Lastly, I respectfully submit that Mr. Jordan remove his shirt to see if there are any Nazi tatoos on HIS torso. While I have heard of a genocide being launched against Gaza, I have seen no evidence of it, apart from Hezbollah propaganda, despite the fact that every mainstream media outlet is slavering for it and would blow it out of all proportion if it was being implemented. While three of the candidates here seem to be Mamdani wanna-bes, Jordan seems the least capable (he couldn't even memorize his opening statement?!), and, therefore, will be most appealing to the Democrat base in Maine. Of the four here, Mr. Jackson seems the least radical and, therefore, his chances are slim to none among the socialist wing of his party.
